---
title: "{{< logo >}} Manual"
author:
  - name: "Paolo Bosetti"
    affiliations: 
      - name: "University of Trento"
date: 07/07/2026
date-modified: today
format: html
---

# Contents

## MADS commands

<!-- LIST of all MADS executable commands in the standard installation,
     each linking to a separate manual page -->

* [mads](mads.qmd) — the command dispatcher and its built-in subcommands
* [broker](broker.qmd) — the ZeroMQ proxy and settings service at the hub of a MADS network
* [plugin](plugin.qmd) — scaffold a new plugin, or migrate an existing one to a newer protocol
* [source](source-filter-sink.qmd), [filter](source-filter-sink.qmd), [sink](source-filter-sink.qmd) — the three generic plugin-loader agents
* [logger](logger.qmd) — persists every topic to MongoDB and/or a file
* [feedback](feedback-perf_assess.qmd), [perf_assess](feedback-perf_assess.qmd) — a generic subscriber and publisher for testing, plus the default plugins
* [inspect_plugin](inspect_plugin.qmd) — diagnose a compiled `.plugin` file (protocol version, ABI mismatches)
* [federate](federate.qmd) — relay selected topics between two independent MADS networks
* [fsm](fsm.qmd) — generate a finite-state-machine agent skeleton from a Graphviz description
* [package](package.qmd) — list, inspect, and install pre-compiled community packages
* [dealer](dealer-worker.qmd), [worker](dealer-worker.qmd) — round-robin load balancing across multiple worker processes
* [rsource](rust-loaders.qmd), [rfilter](rust-loaders.qmd), [rsink](rust-loaders.qmd) — Rust-plugin loaders (not on Windows)


## MADS concepts

<!-- Each of the following must link to a separate manual page -->

* [Network discovery](network-discovery.qmd)
* [Message routing and topics](message-routing.qmd)
* [Plugins and extensions](plugins-extensions.qmd)
* [Timing and scheduling](timing-scheduling.qmd)
* [Message queues, blocking/non-blocking behavior](message-queues.qmd)
* [Databases and data storage](databases-storage.qmd)
* [Cryptography and security](cryptography-security.qmd)
